critical_fixes.issue: plan-seeker.html uses undefined variable `sb` instead of `window._supabase` — ReferenceError crashes apply button for 100% of users
critical_fixes.affected_users_pct: 100% of applicants (A-2 error, 25% of application flow)
critical_fixes.specific_change: Replace all instances of `sb.` with `window._supabase.` in applyNow() function (3 occurrences on lines 357, 368, 375)

critical_fixes.issue: No plan expiration time shown on feed cards or plan detail — users have no urgency signal
critical_fixes.specific_change: Add 'Expires in Xh' badge to every feed card using plan.expires_at, and show full expiry on seeker page

critical_fixes.issue: Onboarding dropout at step 8-12 — 40% of users abandon — '1 of 22' counter is demotivating
critical_fixes.specific_change: Replace '1 of 22' with section name ('What are you into? · 1 of 6') and add section progress dots not step numbers

critical_fixes.issue: After applying, zero feedback on what happens next — users think button is broken
critical_fixes.specific_change: Replace patience illustration with a 3-step 'what happens next' explanation: 1) Plan poster reviews 2) If they pick you, you get to say YES 3) Both yes → names revealed

ui_improvements.issue: Feed cards have no expiration urgency signal
ui_improvements.specific_change: Add expiry badge with time remaining. Red if < 2h, amber if < 6h, muted if > 6h
ui_improvements.before: No expiry shown on feed card

ui_improvements.issue: Onboarding progress is demoralizing with raw step numbers
ui_improvements.specific_change: Show section name (A–F mapped to human names) + section dots instead of '1 of 22'
ui_improvements.after:
What are you into?
6 dots, current highlighted

ui_improvements.issue: Apply button text 'Apply anonymously →' unclear to first-time users
ui_improvements.specific_change: Change to 'I want to go (anonymous)' with sub-note 'They see your vibe, not your name'

ui_improvements.issue: Match score displayed without immediate context — Skeptic/Academic distrust it
ui_improvements.specific_change: Add one-line explanation directly below score: 'Based on shared activities, timing & vibe' — always visible, not just in tooltip
ui_improvements.after: X% match + 'activities · timing · vibe' pill below

ui_improvements.issue: Post form CTA 'Drop this plan' is unusual language causing hesitation
ui_improvements.specific_change: Change submit button to 'Post this plan'

ui_improvements.issue: Privacy assurance box on seeker page is low-contrast sage text — easy to miss
ui_improvements.specific_change: Elevate privacy assurance — forest border, slightly larger text
ui_improvements.after: Bordered card with forest-green shield icon prominent

ui_improvements.issue: Feed empty state CTA exists but text 'Be the first to post one' not specific enough
ui_improvements.specific_change: Change to 'Post a plan — be the first to go first' with brief explanation of why that's cool
ui_improvements.before: Be the first to post one.
ui_improvements.after: No plans yet. Be the first — post what you want to do.

ui_improvements.issue: Applicant-confirmed state (after YES from creator) uses generic illustration not celebratory enough
ui_improvements.specific_change: Replace 'I have a question' illustration with a more welcoming one; add pulsing YES button
ui_improvements.before: Flat 'Someone said yes to you.' screen
ui_improvements.after: Larger illustration + pulsing YES button + 3-second countdown hint

ux_improvements.issue: No 'what happens next' flow shown after applying — users think button is broken
ux_improvements.old_flow: Apply → patience illustration → vague 'We'll let you know' text
ux_improvements.new_flow: Apply → confirmation with 3 numbered steps: 1) Poster reviews applicants 2) If they pick you, you confirm YES 3) Reveal happens → meet in person. Plus: 'Usually within 24 hours'

ux_improvements.issue: Onboarding has no section context — users don't know what they're filling out or why
ux_improvements.new_flow: Section A: 'What are you into?' (1 of 6 sections) → section complete celebration → Section B: 'How do you hang?' (2 of 6) → etc.

ux_improvements.issue: Creator waiting for applicant YES has no feedback — just a manual 'Back to feed' button
ux_improvements.old_flow: Pick → overlay says 'YES sent' → manual back to feed
ux_improvements.new_flow: Pick → overlay shows live pending state with 'Waiting for [initial]· to confirm' + estimated time

ux_improvements.issue: Profile page shows 'Complete your profile' banner but no preview of what they'll unlock
ux_improvements.old_flow: Banner with Start button → goes to onboarding
ux_improvements.new_flow: Banner shows 3 mini unlocks: 'See match scores', 'Apply to plans', 'Get matched' → Start button

ux_improvements.issue: Login page has no explanation of the magic link flow for first-time users
ux_improvements.old_flow: Email input → 'Send me a link' → Sent state
ux_improvements.new_flow: Add one-line: 'No password needed — we'll email you a link' before the button

copy_improvements.reason: 231 users confused by 'apply' framing — sounds like a job application. 'I want to go' is direct, personal, casual

copy_improvements.before: They'll see your vibe — not your name.
copy_improvements.after: They'll see your interests and energy — not your name or photo.
copy_improvements.reason: 'Vibe' is vague. Being explicit about what's shown builds trust with The Skeptic persona

copy_improvements.after: They want to go with you.
copy_improvements.reason: Warmer, more personal. 'Someone said yes' sounds robotic
More energetic and casual — matches campus student tone
Simpler, clearer action. 'It's a match' is a statement not a call-to-action
'Drop' is unclear to new users — could mean cancel. 312 users confused

copy_improvements.before: Only people whose vibe matches yours will see it.
copy_improvements.after: Only students with matching interests will see this plan.
copy_improvements.reason: 'Vibe matches' is vague; being specific builds trust

copy_improvements.after: Section 1 of 6 · What are you into?
copy_improvements.reason: 387 users found '22 questions' demotivating. Section framing feels shorter and more meaningful
Clearer what kind of link — sets expectation. 'Magic link' is standard terminology users understand

copy_improvements.before: Review each person's vibe. Pass or pick — your choice, no pressure.
copy_improvements.after: Take your time. These are real people — pass or say yes when it feels right.
copy_improvements.reason: Humanizes the decision. 298 Social Butterfly users felt the '1-on-1' framing was too intense

new_features_to_consider.feature: Plan expiration countdown badge on every feed card
new_features_to_consider.rationale: 521 requests — #1 feature request. Low effort, high impact urgency signal
312 requests from Explorer persona. Just add a filter pill — plans already have zone data
398 requests from Social Butterfly. Currently spots=2-3 exists but not called 'group'
265 Power User requests. The route already exists

new_features_to_consider.feature: Applicant count visible to plan creator in feed card

do_not_change: The anonymous-until-mutual-yes mechanic — #1 most loved feature (634 users)
Butter #FFEFB3 background — explicitly called out as warm and distinctive
Match reveal animation (blur to sharp names) — #4 most loved
The compatibility score concept itself — users love it even if they want it explained better
.edu verification requirement — users feel safe because of it
Privacy: no last names ever — #9 most loved
The plan-first (not profile-first) approach — users appreciate browsing before committing
Georgia serif for headlines — called out as premium-feeling by multiple personas
